d7b_reasoning: The strategy exhibits coherent mean-reversion logic: entering on Monday when RSI indicates oversold conditions (RSI < 40), price trades below its moving average, and recent returns are negative. The dual exit paths are sensible—either technical recovery (RSI > 55 with SMA crossover) or time-based profit-taking mid-week. Theme alignment is strong as day_of_week is central to both entry and exit logic, anchoring trades to calendar patterns (Monday dips, Wednesday exits). However, the factor set {close, day_of_week, return_1h, return_24h, rsi_14, sma_20} appears nearly identical to prior #10, differing only in threshold values (RSI 40 vs potentially different cutoffs, return_24h -0.02 vs other thresholds). This represents a shallow variant: the same structural composition with operator/threshold adjustments rather than novel factor combinations. The economic story is defensible but the factor architecture is not sufficiently distinct from existing hypotheses in the batch.
